name: Amaretto Hazelnut Macaroon Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 0:30
1. Heat oven to 350°F. Grease a 10-inch springform pan and line with parchment paper (do not use wax paper as it sticks horribly). Grease the parchment.
2. Line a cookie sheet with greased parchment.
3. Whisk egg whites and vanilla together. Remove as much skin from hazelnuts as convenient, chop in food processor with 1 cup powdered sugar for 30 seconds. Add both powdered and regular sugar, pulse a few times to combine. With the processor running, pour in egg mixture.
4. Reserve half of the batter. Pour remaining into springform pan, smooth with spatula. Pour reserved batter onto cookie sheet, spread into a 7-8 inch disk.
5. Bake crust for 25-30 minutes (disk for 20-25 minutes). Cool on wire rack.
6. Chop disc into 1/8 inch pieces and soak in 1/4 amaretto (do not soak for more than 15 min or they get soggy).
7. Carefully remove crust (very carefully). Replace bottom of springform with foil-wrapped cardboard circle. Replace crust.
8. Amaretto cheesecake filling:
- Sprinkle gelatin over 1/4 cup amaretto, let stand 5 minutes.
- Heat in saucepan with hot (not boiling) water, stirring for 4 minutes. Keep warm.
- Beat cream cheese in mixer for 1 minute. Add lemon juice and zest, mix. Beat to soft peaks. Fold 1/3 of cream into cream cheese. Fold in remaining whipped cream. Fold in soaked macaroon disk bits.
9. Scrape into prepared pan, cover with plastic wrap. Refrigerate at least 3 hours (preferably overnight).
